codeReview.summary: The fix commit addresses all prior blocking scrutiny findings from mcp-write-tools and mcp-remote-mode: local resolve/delete now return protocol-level McpError on not-found, remote spike list parsing now supports hosted API {data:[...], next_cursor} shape, remote resolve/delete no longer call non-existent worker routes, submit_spike_remote always provides projectKey, and HTTP tools/list assertions are now unconditional.

issues.description: New fetch_remote_spikes parsing tests validate the parsing expression on inline JSON values rather than exercising fetch_remote_spikes end-to-end with HTTP response fixtures/mocks, so regression protection for request/response integration remains lighter than it could be.

sharedStateObservations.observation: worker-cli skill still appears partially out of sync with practical execution for this feature: transcript shows implementation edits before tests and no explicit cargo build step, despite the skill requiring tests-first and a build check.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-cli SKILL.md procedure steps 3 and 7 vs transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 35b88dcb-0a8b-4050-84b8-29242d50441d (multiple Edit operations before new tests and verification commands listing cargo test/clippy/manual MCP check but no cargo build).

summary: PASS: The mcp-scrutiny-fixes implementation adequately resolves the previously blocking failures in both mcp-write-tools and mcp-remote-mode, and aligns remote-mode behavior with the hosted API contract for spike list shape and unsupported mutation routes.
